subject: GRB 180407A: COATLI Upper Limits
submitter: Alan M. Watson at Instituto de Astronomia UNAM  <alan@astro.unam.mx>
body:
Alan M. Watson (UNAM), Nat Butler (ASU), Rosa L. Becerra (UNAM), William
H. Lee (UNAM), Carlos Romïŋ―ïŋ―n-Zïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―ïŋ―iga (UNAM), Alexander Kutyrev (GSFC), and
Eleonora Troja (GSFC), report:

We observed the field of GRB 180407A (Deich et al., GCN 22612) with the
COATLI 50-cm telescope and interim imager
(http://coatli.astroscu.unam.mx) at the Observatorio Astronïŋ―ïŋ―mico
Nacional on the Sierra de San Pedro Mïŋ―ïŋ―rtir from 2018-04-07 02:26:12 to
03:01:38 (0.52 to 1.12 hours after trigger) obtaining a total of 510
seconds of exposure in the w filter.

The images were obtained at high airmass (above 3.1) and during
twilight.

For a source within the Swift/BAT error circle, in comparison with the
USNO-B1 catalog, we obtain the following 10-sigma upper limit:

w > 16.53

This magnitude is in the USNO system and is not corrected for Galactic
extinction in the direction of the GRB.

We thank the COATLI technical team (Fernando ïŋ―ïŋ―ngeles, Oscar Chapa,
Salvador Cuevas, Alejandro Farah, Jorge Fuentes, Rosalïŋ―ïŋ―a Langarica,
Fernando Quirïŋ―ïŋ―s, and Carlos Tejada) and the staff of the Observatorio
Astronïŋ―ïŋ―mico Nacional.


-- 
Dr. Alan M. Watson
